steps.title: Tangent perpendicular to radius - find the radius slope
steps.content: A tangent line is always perpendicular to the radius at the point of tangency. The radius goes from center $(3, 8)$ to point $(a, -5)$, giving slope: $$m_{\text{radius}} = \frac{-5 - 8}{a - 3} = \frac{-13}{a - 3}.$$

steps.title: Use the negative-reciprocal rule and solve
steps.content: For perpendicular lines, $m_{\text{radius}} = -\frac{1}{m_{\text{tangent}}} = -\frac{7}{4}$. Set up: $$\frac{-13}{a - 3} = -\frac{7}{4} \implies 7(a - 3) = 52 \implies a = \frac{73}{7}.$$ That confirms D is correct.
